article_title: Amazon to Buy Globalstar for $11.57 Billion in Satellite Push That Pulls Apple Into Its Orbit

content_preview.title: Amazon to Buy Globalstar for $11.57 Billion in Satellite Push That Pulls Apple Into Its Orbit
content_preview.summary: Amazon will acquire Globalstar for about $11.57 billion and inherit the company's iPhone satellite partnership, accelerating its Starlink challenge and its rebranded Amazon Leo constellation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amazon announced on April 14 that it has agreed to acquire satellite operator Globalstar in a deal valued at roughly $11.57 billion, folding a legacy mobile-satellite provider and its iPhone connectivity partnership into the e-commerce giant's rebranded Amazon Leo constellation. The transaction, [reported by CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/14/amazon-globalstar-satellite-leo-internet.html), marks Amazon's most aggressive move yet to challenge SpaceX's Starlink and gives it an imme...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong M&A/space business article on Amazon's Globalstar acquisition.
editor_notes.source_verification: TechCrunch VERIFIED via WebFetch: $11.57B deal, $90/share all-cash, Amazon Leo rebrand, Apple iPhone/Watch satellite partnership inherited, Emergency SOS continuation, Starlink 10,000+ vs Amazon ~200/3200 planned, 2028 direct-to-device launch all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All deal terms and competitive context verified.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Globalstar or Amazon Leo acquisition coverage.
